description: Verdict-composition cases whose expected output is defined by kernel spec §6.2 (Abort > Pause > Modify > Retry > Proceed, first-objection-wins) and §6.1 (Modify composes multiple transforms in registration order). Each `expected` value was computed by invoking kernel.verdicts.compose against the TypeScript reference implementation (typescript/kernel/runtime/src/lib/runtime-kernel.ts) and is committed verbatim as cross-language authority; the generator script is tooling, not the oracle.

single-proceed.description: A single Proceed verdict composes to Proceed: no objection is raised at any priority level.

all-proceed.description: Multiple Proceed verdicts and no objection of any kind still compose to Proceed.

single-modify.description: A single Modify verdict composes to Modify carrying that verdict's transform unchanged (not wrapped in an array) per §6.1.

multi-modify-preserves-registration-orde.description: Two Modify verdicts, with an interleaved Proceed, compose to a single Modify whose transform is the array of the individual transforms in registration order (§6.1: 'the kernel composes multiple transforms in registration order').

abort-dominates-pause-modify-retry.description: Abort outranks every other verdict kind under the fixed priority order Abort > Pause > Modify > Retry > Proceed (§6.2), regardless of the verdicts' position in the input array.

pause-dominates-modify-retry-proceed.description: Pause outranks Modify, Retry, and Proceed under §6.2's priority order when no Abort is present.

modify-dominates-retry-proceed.description: Modify outranks Retry and Proceed under §6.2's priority order when no Abort or Pause is present.

retry-dominates-proceed.description: Retry outranks Proceed under §6.2's priority order when no Abort, Pause, or Modify is present.
